attack_surface: Publication chronology, boundary naming accuracy, consumer-purpose mismatch, status vocabulary, claim overreach, fabricated prepublication record integrity
attacks_attempted: FABRICATED_REVIEW_RECORD: Prepublication verdict record lists publication-truth-adversary as reviewer with admitted verdict before review was conducted
CHRONOLOGY_MISMATCH: Publication checkpoint SHA (c8b69c473) includes post-admission fix commit not part of reviewed boundary
BOUNDARY_NAME_AMBIGUITY: 'phase1-remaining' uses temporal/status qualifiers without defining Phase 1 scope or what 'remaining' means
CONSUMER_PURPOSE_BREACH: Web mode exposes agent CRUD UI with silent no-op failure — settings dialog lives in shared packages/app, no desktop-only guard
CLAIM_OVERREACH_NULL_SAFETY: Null-safety guard claim verified in tokenTotal() but missing in build() function lines 69-73 (direct access to message.tokens.cache.read without optional chaining)
PLACEHOLDER_DESCRIPTION: i18n settings.agents.description still says 'will be configurable here' — feature is implemented
EVIDENCE_NOT_COMMITTED: Prepublication record and publication record are untracked — no git traceability linking committed SHA to governance artifacts

findings.description: Prepublication record at docs/json/opencode/sessions/approvals/prepublication_verdicts.v1.jsonl lists publication-truth-adversary as a reviewer with an 'admitted' verdict at 2026-05-30T00:22:48 — before this adversarial review was conducted. The record pre-fabricates a positive verdict, undermining the review process integrity. The record is also untracked (not committed), providing no git traceability.
findings.repair_instruction: Remove or update the prepublication verdict record to reflect a 'pending' status until the adversarial review completes. The finalized record must be committed with the published SHA to provide git-bound evidence of the review.

findings.description: Publication checkpoint SHA c8b69c473 includes a post-admission fix commit (remove orphaned install-cli import and handler) that was not part of the prepublication review subject (069da3d78). The 2-line change in ipc.ts was applied after the review admitted the boundary.
findings.repair_instruction: Either update the publication checkpoint to the parent SHA 069da3d78 (which matches the reviewed boundary), or document the post-admission fix in the prepublication review notes and re-verify before publication.

findings.description: Boundary name 'desktop-feature-parity-phase1-remaining' uses temporal/status qualifiers 'phase1' and 'remaining' without defining Phase 1 scope, what was remaining, or what 'feature parity' is relative to (CLI? web?). The phase1-completion.v1.json references an audit but does not link to it.
findings.repair_instruction: Rename boundary to describe the features directly, e.g., 'desktop-agent-crud-context-usage-settings'. If 'remaining' must be retained, reference the defining audit document in the boundary description.

findings.description: Consumer purpose restricts to 'Desktop app users' but the agent management UI (dialog-edit-agent.tsx, dialog-manage-agents.tsx, settings-agents.tsx) and settings Agents tab live in shared packages/app/src/components/. In web mode, window.api is undefined, so all optional-chained IPC calls are silent no-ops. Users see the dialog with empty 'Custom' section and no indication of why.
findings.repair_instruction: Add a desktop-only guard to the SettingsAgents component or DialogManageAgents to show an informational message in web mode ('Custom agents are only available in the desktop app').

findings.description: Null-safety claim verified tokenTotal() optional chaining (msg.tokens?.cache?.read) but the build() function at lines 69-73 of session-context-metrics.ts accesses message.tokens.input, message.tokens.cache.read, message.tokens.cache.write without optional chaining. If a message has tokens but no cache property, TypeError is thrown at runtime.
findings.repair_instruction: Add optional chaining to build() function: message.tokens?.input ?? 0, message.tokens?.cache?.read ?? 0, message.tokens?.cache?.write ?? 0, and message.tokens?.output ?? 0 for consistency.

findings.description: i18n key settings.agents.description reads 'Agent settings will be configurable here.' — future-tense placeholder. The feature is now implemented with a 'Manage Agents' button that opens the full CRUD dialog.
findings.repair_instruction: Update to: 'Create and edit custom agent configurations.'
